/**
 * Error Recovery Service
 * Implements error recovery strategies including exponential backoff,
 * circuit breaker patterns, and error classification
 */

import { logger } from './loggerService';
import { ErrorCode } from '../constants/errorCodes';

/**
 * Error Type Classification
 */
export enum ErrorType {
  TRANSIENT = 'TRANSIENT', // Temporary errors that can be retried
  PERMANENT = 'PERMANENT', // Permanent errors that should not be retried
  RATE_LIMIT = 'RATE_LIMIT', // Rate limiting errors
  TIMEOUT = 'TIMEOUT', // Timeout errors
  NETWORK = 'NETWORK', // Network connectivity errors
  AUTHENTICATION = 'AUTHENTICATION', // Auth errors
  VALIDATION = 'VALIDATION', // Validation errors
  UNKNOWN = 'UNKNOWN', // Unknown errors
}

/**
 * Error Classification Result
 */
export interface ErrorClassification {
  type: ErrorType;
  isRetryable: boolean;
  suggestedDelay: number; // milliseconds
  maxRetries: number;
  errorCode?: ErrorCode;
}

/**
 * Retry Strategy Configuration
 */
export interface RetryConfig {
  maxRetries: number;
  initialDelay: number; // milliseconds
  maxDelay: number; // milliseconds
  backoffMultiplier: number;
  jitterFactor: number; // 0-1, adds randomness to delay
}

/**
 * Circuit Breaker State
 */
export enum CircuitState {
  CLOSED = 'CLOSED', // Normal operation
  OPEN = 'OPEN', // Circuit is open, requests fail immediately
  HALF_OPEN = 'HALF_OPEN', // Testing if service recovered
}

/**
 * Circuit Breaker Configuration
 */
export interface CircuitBreakerConfig {
  failureThreshold: number; // Number of failures before opening
  successThreshold: number; // Number of successes to close from half-open
  timeout: number; // Time to wait before half-open (milliseconds)
  monitoringPeriod: number; // Period to track failures (milliseconds)
}

/**
 * Circuit Breaker Instance
 */
class CircuitBreaker {
  private state: CircuitState = CircuitState.CLOSED;
  private failureCount: number = 0;
  private successCount: number = 0;
  private lastFailureTime?: number;
  private failures: number[] = []; // Timestamps of failures

  constructor(
    private name: string,
    private config: CircuitBreakerConfig
  ) {}

  /**
   * Check if request can proceed
   */
  canProceed(): boolean {
    this.cleanOldFailures();

    if (this.state === CircuitState.OPEN) {
      // Check if timeout has passed
      if (
        this.lastFailureTime &&
        Date.now() - this.lastFailureTime >= this.config.timeout
      ) {
        logger.info('Circuit breaker transitioning to half-open', {
          name: this.name,
          failureCount: this.failureCount,
        });
        this.state = CircuitState.HALF_OPEN;
        this.successCount = 0;
        return true;
      }
      return false;
    }

    return true;
  }

  /**
   * Record successful operation
   */
  recordSuccess(): void {
    if (this.state === CircuitState.HALF_OPEN) {
      this.successCount++;
      if (this.successCount >= this.config.successThreshold) {
        logger.info('Circuit breaker closing', {
          name: this.name,
          successCount: this.successCount,
        });
        this.state = CircuitState.CLOSED;
        this.failureCount = 0;
        this.failures = [];
      }
    } else if (this.state === CircuitState.CLOSED) {
      // Reset failure count on success
      this.failureCount = 0;
      this.failures = [];
    }
  }

  /**
   * Record failed operation
   */
  recordFailure(): void {
    const now = Date.now();
    this.failures.push(now);
    this.lastFailureTime = now;
    this.failureCount++;

    if (this.state === CircuitState.HALF_OPEN) {
      logger.warn('Circuit breaker opening from half-open', {
        name: this.name,
      });
      this.state = CircuitState.OPEN;
      this.successCount = 0;
    } else if (this.state === CircuitState.CLOSED) {
      this.cleanOldFailures();
      if (this.failures.length >= this.config.failureThreshold) {
        logger.warn('Circuit breaker opening', {
          name: this.name,
          failureCount: this.failureCount,
          threshold: this.config.failureThreshold,
        });
        this.state = CircuitState.OPEN;
      }
    }
  }

  /**
   * Clean old failures outside monitoring period
   */
  private cleanOldFailures(): void {
    const cutoff = Date.now() - this.config.monitoringPeriod;
    this.failures = this.failures.filter(timestamp => timestamp > cutoff);
  }

  /**
   * Get current state
   */
  getState(): CircuitState {
    return this.state;
  }

  /**
   * Get statistics
   */
  getStats(): {
    state: CircuitState;
    failureCount: number;
    successCount: number;
    recentFailures: number;
  } {
    this.cleanOldFailures();
    return {
      state: this.state,
      failureCount: this.failureCount,
      successCount: this.successCount,
      recentFailures: this.failures.length,
    };
  }

  /**
   * Reset circuit breaker
   */
  reset(): void {
    this.state = CircuitState.CLOSED;
    this.failureCount = 0;
    this.successCount = 0;
    this.failures = [];
    this.lastFailureTime = undefined;
    logger.info('Circuit breaker reset', { name: this.name });
  }
}

/**
 * Error Recovery Service
 */
export class ErrorRecoveryService {
  private circuitBreakers: Map<string, CircuitBreaker> = new Map();
  private defaultRetryConfig: RetryConfig = {
    maxRetries: 3,
    initialDelay: 1000,
    maxDelay: 30000,
    backoffMultiplier: 2,
    jitterFactor: 0.1,
  };
  private defaultCircuitBreakerConfig: CircuitBreakerConfig = {
    failureThreshold: 5,
    successThreshold: 2,
    timeout: 60000, // 1 minute
    monitoringPeriod: 120000, // 2 minutes
  };

  /**
   * Classify error type
   */
  classifyError(error: any): ErrorClassification {
    // Extract error information
    const errorMessage = error?.message || String(error);
    const errorCode = error?.code as ErrorCode | undefined;
    const statusCode = error?.statusCode || error?.status;

    // Network errors (transient)
    if (
      errorMessage.includes('ECONNREFUSED') ||
      errorMessage.includes('ENOTFOUND') ||
      errorMessage.includes('ETIMEDOUT') ||
      errorMessage.includes('ECONNRESET') ||
      errorMessage.includes('network') ||
      errorMessage.includes('fetch failed')
    ) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.NETWORK,
        isRetryable: true,
        suggestedDelay: 2000,
        maxRetries: 5,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Timeout errors (transient)
    if (
      errorMessage.includes('timeout') ||
      errorMessage.includes('timed out') ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.SCRAPER_TIMEOUT ||
      statusCode === 504
    ) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.TIMEOUT,
        isRetryable: true,
        suggestedDelay: 3000,
        maxRetries: 3,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Rate limiting errors (transient with longer delay)
    if (
      errorMessage.includes('rate limit') ||
      errorMessage.includes('too many requests') ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.SCRAPER_RATE_LIMITED ||
      statusCode === 429
    ) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.RATE_LIMIT,
        isRetryable: true,
        suggestedDelay: 60000, // 1 minute
        maxRetries: 3,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Authentication errors (permanent)
    if (
      errorCode === ErrorCode.AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.AUTH_TOKEN_INVALID ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.AUTH_USER_BLOCKED ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.UNAUTHORIZED ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.FORBIDDEN ||
      statusCode === 401 ||
      statusCode === 403
    ) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.AUTHENTICATION,
        isRetryable: false,
        suggestedDelay: 0,
        maxRetries: 0,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Validation errors (permanent)
    if (
      errorCode === ErrorCode.VALIDATION_ERROR ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.SCRAPER_INVALID_CONFIG ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.BAD_REQUEST ||
      statusCode === 400
    ) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.VALIDATION,
        isRetryable: false,
        suggestedDelay: 0,
        maxRetries: 0,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Not found errors (permanent)
    if (
      errorCode === ErrorCode.NOT_FOUND ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.SCRAPER_SOURCE_NOT_FOUND ||
      statusCode === 404
    ) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.PERMANENT,
        isRetryable: false,
        suggestedDelay: 0,
        maxRetries: 0,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Server errors (transient)
    if (statusCode >= 500 && statusCode < 600) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.TRANSIENT,
        isRetryable: true,
        suggestedDelay: 5000,
        maxRetries: 3,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Database errors (transient)
    if (
      errorCode === ErrorCode.DATABASE_CONNECTION_ERROR ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.DATABASE_QUERY_ERROR ||
      errorCode === ErrorCode.DATABASE_TRANSACTION_ERROR
    ) {
      return {
        type: ErrorType.TRANSIENT,
        isRetryable: true,
        suggestedDelay: 2000,
        maxRetries: 3,
        errorCode,
      };
    }

    // Default: unknown error (cautiously retryable)
    return {
      type: ErrorType.UNKNOWN,
      isRetryable: true,
      suggestedDelay: 1000,
      maxRetries: 2,
      errorCode,
    };
  }

  /**
   * Calculate retry delay with exponential backoff
   */
  calculateRetryDelay(
    attemptNumber: number,
    config: RetryConfig = this.defaultRetryConfig
  ): number {
    // Calculate exponential backoff
    const exponentialDelay =
      config.initialDelay * Math.pow(config.backoffMultiplier, attemptNumber);

    // Cap at max delay
    const cappedDelay = Math.min(exponentialDelay, config.maxDelay);

    // Add jitter to prevent thundering herd
    const jitter = cappedDelay * config.jitterFactor * (Math.random() - 0.5);
    const finalDelay = Math.max(0, cappedDelay + jitter);

    return Math.round(finalDelay);
  }

  /**
   * Execute operation with retry logic
   */
  async executeWithRetry<T>(
    operation: () => Promise<T>,
    options?: {
      operationName?: string;
      retryConfig?: Partial<RetryConfig>;
      onRetry?: (error: any, attempt: number, delay: number) => void;
      shouldRetry?: (error: any, attempt: number) => boolean;
    }
  ): Promise<T> {
    const config: RetryConfig = {
      ...this.defaultRetryConfig,
      ...options?.retryConfig,
    };

    let lastError: any;
    let attempt = 0;

    while (attempt <= config.maxRetries) {
      try {
        const result = await operation();
        
        // Log success if this was a retry
        if (attempt > 0) {
          logger.info('Operation succeeded after retry', {
            operationName: options?.operationName,
            attempt,
            totalAttempts: attempt + 1,
          });
        }

        return result;
      } catch (error) {
        lastError = error;
        attempt++;

        // Classify error
        const classification = this.classifyError(error);

        // Check if we should retry
        const shouldRetry =
          options?.shouldRetry?.(error, attempt) ??
          (classification.isRetryable && attempt <= config.maxRetries);

        if (!shouldRetry) {
          logger.error(
            'Operation failed, not retrying',
            error as Error,
            {
              operationName: options?.operationName,
              attempt,
              errorType: classification.type,
              isRetryable: classification.isRetryable,
            }
          );
          throw error;
        }

        // Calculate delay
        const delay = Math.max(
          this.calculateRetryDelay(attempt - 1, config),
          classification.suggestedDelay
        );

        logger.warn('Operation failed, will retry', {
          operationName: options?.operationName,
          attempt,
          maxRetries: config.maxRetries,
          delay,
          errorType: classification.type,
          error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
        });

        // Call retry callback
        options?.onRetry?.(error, attempt, delay);

        // Wait before retry
        await this.sleep(delay);
      }
    }

    // All retries exhausted
    logger.error(
      'Operation failed after all retries',
      lastError as Error,
      {
        operationName: options?.operationName,
        totalAttempts: attempt,
        maxRetries: config.maxRetries,
      }
    );

    throw lastError;
  }

  /**
   * Execute operation with circuit breaker
   */
  async executeWithCircuitBreaker<T>(
    operation: () => Promise<T>,
    options: {
      breakerName: string;
      circuitBreakerConfig?: Partial<CircuitBreakerConfig>;
      fallback?: () => Promise<T>;
    }
  ): Promise<T> {
    // Get or create circuit breaker
    let breaker = this.circuitBreakers.get(options.breakerName);
    if (!breaker) {
      const config: CircuitBreakerConfig = {
        ...this.defaultCircuitBreakerConfig,
        ...options.circuitBreakerConfig,
      };
      breaker = new CircuitBreaker(options.breakerName, config);
      this.circuitBreakers.set(options.breakerName, breaker);
    }

    // Check if circuit is open
    if (!breaker.canProceed()) {
      logger.warn('Circuit breaker is open, request rejected', {
        breakerName: options.breakerName,
        state: breaker.getState(),
      });

      // Use fallback if available
      if (options.fallback) {
        logger.info('Using fallback for circuit breaker', {
          breakerName: options.breakerName,
        });
        return await options.fallback();
      }

      throw new Error(
        `Circuit breaker "${options.breakerName}" is open. Service temporarily unavailable.`
      );
    }

    try {
      const result = await operation();
      breaker.recordSuccess();
      return result;
    } catch (error) {
      breaker.recordFailure();
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Execute operation with both retry and circuit breaker
   */
  async executeWithRecovery<T>(
    operation: () => Promise<T>,
    options: {
      operationName: string;
      breakerName: string;
      retryConfig?: Partial<RetryConfig>;
      circuitBreakerConfig?: Partial<CircuitBreakerConfig>;
      fallback?: () => Promise<T>;
      onRetry?: (error: any, attempt: number, delay: number) => void;
    }
  ): Promise<T> {
    return this.executeWithCircuitBreaker(
      () =>
        this.executeWithRetry(operation, {
          operationName: options.operationName,
          retryConfig: options.retryConfig,
          onRetry: options.onRetry,
        }),
      {
        breakerName: options.breakerName,
        circuitBreakerConfig: options.circuitBreakerConfig,
        fallback: options.fallback,
      }
    );
  }

  /**
   * Get circuit breaker by name
   */
  getCircuitBreaker(name: string): CircuitBreaker | undefined {
    return this.circuitBreakers.get(name);
  }

  /**
   * Get all circuit breaker stats
   */
  getAllCircuitBreakerStats(): Record<
    string,
    {
      state: CircuitState;
      failureCount: number;
      successCount: number;
      recentFailures: number;
    }
  > {
    const stats: Record<string, any> = {};
    for (const [name, breaker] of this.circuitBreakers.entries()) {
      stats[name] = breaker.getStats();
    }
    return stats;
  }

  /**
   * Reset circuit breaker
   */
  resetCircuitBreaker(name: string): void {
    const breaker = this.circuitBreakers.get(name);
    if (breaker) {
      breaker.reset();
    }
  }

  /**
   * Reset all circuit breakers
   */
  resetAllCircuitBreakers(): void {
    for (const breaker of this.circuitBreakers.values()) {
      breaker.reset();
    }
    logger.info('All circuit breakers reset');
  }

  /**
   * Sleep utility
   */
  private sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
    return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
  }

  /**
   * Check if error is retryable
   */
  isRetryable(error: any): boolean {
    const classification = this.classifyError(error);
    return classification.isRetryable;
  }

  /**
   * Get suggested retry delay for error
   */
  getSuggestedDelay(error: any): number {
    const classification = this.classifyError(error);
    return classification.suggestedDelay;
  }

  /**
   * Get max retries for error
   */
  getMaxRetries(error: any): number {
    const classification = this.classifyError(error);
    return classification.maxRetries;
  }
}

// Export singleton instance
export const errorRecoveryService = new ErrorRecoveryService();

<content>
/**
 * Rate Limiting Middleware
 * Implements rate limiting per endpoint, user, and IP address
 */

import { FastifyRequest, FastifyReply } from 'fastify';
import { logger } from '../services/loggerService';
import { createAppError } from './errorHandler';
import { ErrorCode } from '../constants/errorCodes';

/**
 * Rate limit store interface
 */
interface RateLimitEntry {
  count: number;
  resetTime: number;
}

/**
 * Rate limiter configuration
 */
export interface RateLimiterConfig {
  limit: number; // Maximum requests
  window: number; // Time window in seconds
  keyGenerator?: (request: FastifyRequest) => string; // Custom key generator
  skipSuccessfulRequests?: boolean; // Skip counting successful requests
  skipFailedRequests?: boolean; // Skip counting failed requests
}

/**
 * In-memory rate limit store
 */
class RateLimitStore {
  private store: Map<string, RateLimitEntry> = new Map();
  private cleanupInterval: NodeJS.Timeout;

  constructor() {
    // Cleanup expired entries every minute
    this.cleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {
      this.cleanup();
    }, 60000);
  }

  /**
   * Get current count for key
   */
  get(key: string): RateLimitEntry | undefined {
    const entry = this.store.get(key);

    if (!entry) {
      return undefined;
    }

    // Check if entry has expired
    if (Date.now() > entry.resetTime) {
      this.store.delete(key);
      return undefined;
    }

    return entry;
  }

  /**
   * Increment count for key
   */
  increment(key: string, window: number): RateLimitEntry {
    const now = Date.now();
    const entry = this.store.get(key);

    if (!entry || now > entry.resetTime) {
      // Create new entry
      const newEntry: RateLimitEntry = {
        count: 1,
        resetTime: now + window * 1000,
      };
      this.store.set(key, newEntry);
      return newEntry;
    }

    // Increment existing entry
    entry.count++;
    return entry;
  }

  /**
   * Reset count for key
   */
  reset(key: string): void {
    this.store.delete(key);
  }

  /**
   * Cleanup expired entries
   */
  private cleanup(): void {
    const now = Date.now();
    const keysToDelete: string[] = [];

    for (const [key, entry] of this.store.entries()) {
      if (now > entry.resetTime) {
        keysToDelete.push(key);
      }
    }

    keysToDelete.forEach(key => this.store.delete(key));

    if (keysToDelete.length > 0) {
      logger.debug(`Rate limit cleanup: removed ${keysToDelete.length} expired entries`);
    }
  }

  /**
   * Destroy store and cleanup
   */
  destroy(): void {
    clearInterval(this.cleanupInterval);
    this.store.clear();
  }
}

/**
 * Global rate limit store
 */
const globalStore = new RateLimitStore();

/**
 * Create rate limiter middleware
 */
export function createRateLimiter(config: RateLimiterConfig) {
  return async (request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => {
    // Generate rate limit key
    const key = config.keyGenerator
      ? config.keyGenerator(request)
      : `${request.method}:${request.url}:${request.ip}`;

    // Get current entry
    const entry = globalStore.get(key);

    // Calculate remaining requests
    const remaining = Math.max(0, config.limit - (entry?.count || 0));
    const resetTime = entry?.resetTime || Date.now() + config.window * 1000;

    // Set rate limit headers
    reply.header('X-RateLimit-Limit', config.limit.toString());
    reply.header('X-RateLimit-Remaining', remaining.toString());
    reply.header('X-RateLimit-Reset', Math.ceil(resetTime / 1000).toString());

    // Check if limit exceeded
    if (entry && entry.count >= config.limit) {
      const retryAfter = Math.ceil((entry.resetTime - Date.now()) / 1000);
      reply.header('Retry-After', retryAfter.toString());

      logger.warn('Rate limit exceeded', {
        requestId: request.id,
        key,
        limit: config.limit,
        window: config.window,
        retryAfter,
      });

      throw createAppError(
        'Terlalu banyak request. Silakan coba lagi nanti.',
        ErrorCode.RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED,
        429,
        { retryAfter }
      );
    }

    // Increment counter
    globalStore.increment(key, config.window);
  };
}

/**
 * Rate limit by endpoint
 */
export function rateLimitByEndpoint(limit: number, window: number) {
  return createRateLimiter({
    limit,
    window,
    keyGenerator: (request) => `endpoint:${request.method}:${request.url}`,
  });
}

/**
 * Rate limit by user
 */
export function rateLimitByUser(limit: number, window: number) {
  return createRateLimiter({
    limit,
    window,
    keyGenerator: (request) => {
      const userId = (request.user as any)?.id || 'anonymous';
      return `user:${userId}`;
    },
  });
}

/**
 * Rate limit by IP
 */
export function rateLimitByIP(limit: number, window: number) {
  return createRateLimiter({
    limit,
    window,
    keyGenerator: (request) => `ip:${request.ip}`,
  });
}

/**
 * Rate limit by user and IP (combined)
 */
export function rateLimitByUserAndIP(limit: number, window: number) {
  return createRateLimiter({
    limit,
    window,
    keyGenerator: (request) => {
      const userId = (request.user as any)?.id || 'anonymous';
      return `user-ip:${userId}:${request.ip}`;
    },
  });
}

/**
 * Sliding window rate limiter
 */
export function slidingWindowRateLimiter(limit: number, window: number) {
  return createRateLimiter({
    limit,
    window,
  });
}

/**
 * Burst rate limiter (allows burst but limits over time)
 */
export function burstRateLimiter(
  burstLimit: number,
  sustainedLimit: number,
  window: number
) {
  return async (request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => {
    const key = `burst:${request.method}:${request.url}:${request.ip}`;
    const entry = globalStore.get(key);

    // Check burst limit
    if (entry && entry.count >= burstLimit) {
      // Check if we're still in burst window
      const timeSinceReset = (entry.resetTime - Date.now()) / 1000;
      const expectedCount = Math.ceil((window - timeSinceReset) * (sustainedLimit / window));

      if (entry.count > expectedCount) {
        const retryAfter = Math.ceil((entry.resetTime - Date.now()) / 1000);
        reply.header('Retry-After', retryAfter.toString());

        throw createAppError(
          'Burst limit exceeded',
          ErrorCode.RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED,
          429,
          { retryAfter }
        );
      }
    }

    globalStore.increment(key, window);
  };
}

/**
 * Predefined rate limit configurations
 */
export const rateLimitConfigs = {
  // Auth endpoints - very strict
  auth: {
    register: { limit: 5, window: 3600 }, // 5 per hour
    login: { limit: 10, window: 3600 }, // 10 per hour
    resetPassword: { limit: 3, window: 3600 }, // 3 per hour
  },

  // Admin endpoints - moderate
  admin: {
    default: { limit: 100, window: 3600 }, // 100 per hour
    backup: { limit: 10, window: 3600 }, // 10 per hour
    scraper: { limit: 20, window: 3600 }, // 20 per hour
    analytics: { limit: 50, window: 3600 }, // 50 per hour
  },

  // API endpoints - generous
  api: {
    default: { limit: 1000, window: 3600 }, // 1000 per hour
    search: { limit: 100, window: 60 }, // 100 per minute
    content: { limit: 500, window: 3600 }, // 500 per hour
  },

  // Public endpoints - very generous
  public: {
    default: { limit: 10000, window: 3600 }, // 10000 per hour
  },
};

/**
 * Apply rate limiting to route
 */
export function applyRateLimit(
  request: FastifyRequest,
  config: { limit: number; window: number }
) {
  return createRateLimiter(config);
}

/**
 * Get rate limit status for key
 */
export function getRateLimitStatus(key: string, limit: number) {
  const entry = globalStore.get(key);

  if (!entry) {
    return {
      count: 0,
      limit,
      remaining: limit,
      resetTime: Date.now() + 3600000,
      isLimited: false,
    };
  }

  return {
    count: entry.count,
    limit,
    remaining: Math.max(0, limit - entry.count),
    resetTime: entry.resetTime,
    isLimited: entry.count >= limit,
  };
}

/**
 * Reset rate limit for key
 */
export function resetRateLimit(key: string) {
  globalStore.reset(key);
}

/**
 * Cleanup rate limiter
 */
export function cleanupRateLimiter() {
  globalStore.destroy();
}

/**
 * Rate limit middleware factory
 */
export function createRateLimitMiddleware(
  limitConfig: Record<string, { limit: number; window: number }>
) {
  return async (request: FastifyRequest, reply: FastifyReply) => {
    // Determine which config to use based on route
    let config = limitConfig['default'];

    for (const [pattern, cfg] of Object.entries(limitConfig)) {
      if (pattern !== 'default' && request.url.includes(pattern)) {
        config = cfg;
        break;
      }
    }

    if (config) {
      await createRateLimiter({
        limit: config.limit,
        window: config.window,
        keyGenerator: (req) => `${req.method}:${req.url}:${req.ip}`,
      })(request, reply);
    }
  };
}

<file name="f:\AUDIRA-KOMIK-MDA\apps\gateway\src\services\scraper.ts" language="typescript" >

import axios from 'axios';
import * as cheerio from 'cheerio';
import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';

const prisma = new PrismaClient();

export class ScraperService {
  /**
   * THE REAL ENGINE: Runs a specific scraper source
   */
  async runScraper(sourceId: string): Promise<any> {
    const source = await prisma.scraperSource.findUnique({
      where: { id: sourceId }
    });

    if (!source) throw new Error('Source not found');

    const startTime = Date.now();
    console.log(`[SCRAPER] Starting engine for: ${source.name} (${source.baseUrl})`);

    try {
      // Use cookies from environment or source config
      const scraperCookies = process.env.SCRAPER_COOKIES || '';
      const scraperUA = process.env.SCRAPER_USER_AGENT || 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/147.0.0.0 Safari/537.36';

      const response = await axios.get(source.baseUrl, {
        headers: {
          'User-Agent': scraperUA,
          'Cookie': scraperCookies,
          'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,image/apng,*/*;q=0.8,application/signed-exchange;v=b3;q=0.7',
          'Accept-Language': 'id-ID,id;q=0.9,en-US;q=0.8,en;q=0.7',
          'Referer': 'https://anichin.watch/',
          'Upgrade-Insecure-Requests': '1',
          'Sec-Ch-Ua': '"Not/A)Brand";v="8", "Chromium";v="147", "Google Chrome";v="147"',
          'Sec-Ch-Ua-Mobile': '?0',
          'Sec-Ch-Ua-Platform': '"Windows"',
        },
        timeout: 20000
      });

      const $ = cheerio.load(response.data);
      let createdCount = 0;
      let updatedCount = 0;

      // Anichin Selectors (.bsx for grid items)
      const items = $('.listupd .bsx');
      
      for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
        const el = items[i];
        const linkEl = $(el).find('a.tip');
        const title = linkEl.find('.tt').text().trim() || linkEl.attr('title');
        const link = linkEl.attr('href');
        const imgEl = $(el).find('.limit img');
        const imageUrl = imgEl.attr('data-src') || imgEl.attr('src');
        const episodeInfo = $(el).find('.epx').text().trim();

        if (title && link) {
          const slug = title.toLowerCase().replace(/ /g, '-').replace(/[^\w-]+/g, '');
          const existing = await prisma.content.findUnique({ where: { slug } });
          
          if (!existing) {
            await prisma.content.create({
              data: {
                title,
                slug,
                type: 'DONGHUA',
                status: 'ONGOING',
                imageUrl: imageUrl || '',
                description: `Terbaru: ${episodeInfo}. Auto-Synced from Anichin.`,
                rating: 8.8,
              }
            });
            createdCount++;
          } else {
            updatedCount++;
            await prisma.content.update({
              where: { id: existing.id },
              data: { description: `Update Terkini: ${episodeInfo}.` }
            });
          }
        }
      }

      const duration = ((Date.now() - startTime) / 1000).toFixed(2);

      const log = await prisma.scraperLog.create({
        data: {
          sourceId: source.id,
          status: 'SELESAI',
          itemsFound: items.length,
          itemsAdded: createdCount,
          itemsFailed: 0,
          duration: `${duration}s`
        }
      });

      await prisma.scraperSource.update({
        where: { id: source.id },
        data: { lastScraped: new Date(), status: 'Aktif' }
      });

      return { success: true, log };

    } catch (error: any) {
      const errorMsg = error.response ? `HTTP ${error.response.status}: ${error.message}` : error.message;
      console.error(`[SCRAPER ERROR] ${errorMsg}`);
      
      await prisma.scraperLog.create({
        data: {
          sourceId: source.id,
          status: 'ERROR',
          error: errorMsg,
        }
      });

      await prisma.scraperSource.update({
        where: { id: source.id },
        data: { status: 'Error' }
      });

      throw new Error(errorMsg);
    }
  }

  async scrapeTrending() { return prisma.content.findMany({ take: 5, orderBy: { rating: 'desc' } }); }
  async getSources() { return prisma.scraperSource.findMany({ include: { _count: { select: { logs: true } } }, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } }); }
  async createSource(data: any) { return prisma.scraperSource.create({ data }); }
}

export const scraperService = new ScraperService();
